evidence: The paper evaluates five classification models based on diagnostic performance metrics (AUC, sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV) and economic outcomes, not variable importance. The conclusions focus on model predictive and economic performance: "the random forest model had the best performance with a value of 0.789" and "The decision tree provided the greatest estimated economic benefit when the proportion of high-risk cohorts was lower than 45%." No variable importance values are extracted, reported, or interpreted.
